expectations.text: Response contains required substrings (spikeinterface, WaveformExtractor, pyproject.toml).
expectations.evidence: All three required substrings present throughout response.md.

expectations.text: Response does not contain forbidden substring `pip install -U spyglass`.
expectations.evidence: Forbidden substring absent. Response actively warns against `pip install -U` of SpikeInterface as a fix.

expectations.text: Asks whether the user upgraded any package before naming a fix
expectations.evidence: First section 'did anything in your environment change?' explicitly asks about upgrades to spikeinterface or sibling libraries before any fix.

expectations.text: Identifies the version-coupling issue (WaveformExtractor removed after 0.99.x, SortingAnalyzer replacement)
expectations.evidence: 'SpikeInterface removed the top-level `WaveformExtractor` class after the 0.99.x line and replaced the whole waveform-extraction surface with `SortingAnalyzer` / `create_sorting_analyzer(...)` in 0.100+'.

expectations.text: Points at pyproject.toml as the source of truth for the pin
expectations.evidence: Section 'The pin lives in pyproject.toml, not in the skill' instructs to grep pyproject.toml directly.

expectations.text: Recommends downgrading SpikeInterface, NOT patching Spyglass
expectations.evidence: Fix section: `pip install 'spikeinterface>=0.99.1,<0.100'`. What-not-to-do explicitly: 'Don't patch Spyglass to call SortingAnalyzer'.

expectations.text: If v0 vs v1 mentioned, does not recommend downgrading to v0
expectations.evidence: 'Don't downgrade to the v0 spike-sorting pipeline as a workaround. It doesn't — v0's code paths are also tied to the old SI API ... the answer if you really need a newer SI is to migrate to v1'.
